DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-10-2024 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. PAUL STEINER, Esq. of Law Offices of Paul J. Steiner (tel: 415-981-6100) for Plaintiffs.
Atty. DAMON THURSTON, Esq. of Rankin, Shuey, Mintz, Lampasona & Harper (tel: 510-433-2600) for Defendants.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT DOUGLAS AKAY, AKAY LAW's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' demurrer to plaintiffs' complaint is overruled.

This attorney malpractice case arises from defendants' representation of plaintiffs in an action regarding a cannabis dispensary. Defendants rely on Code of Civil Procedure 340.6 to argue that the malpractice case is time-barred, because the complaint was filed on December 4, 2023 - purportedly more than a year after plaintiffs "suffered the injuries alleged" and "the end of" the "attorney-client relationship." (Memo. 2:3-6.)

This court assumes for argument's sake that alleged injuries were suffered before December 4, 2022. But CCP 340.6 provides that the limitations "period shall be tolled during the time that" the "attorney continues to represent the plaintiff regarding the specific subject matter in which the alleged wrongful act or omission occurred," and defendants' motion to withdraw as counsel was not granted until December 6, 2022. The parties vigorously dispute the facts surrounding defendants' withdrawal. (Memo. 2:25-3:21, 4:23-5:7, 8:3-10:5; Opp. 4:12-5:3, 8:8-9:20.) But factual disputes are not resolved at the pleading stage, particularly in malpractice cases. (Adams v. Paul (1995) 11 Cal.4th 583, 588-89.) Notably, the key case defendants rely on regarded a summary judgment. (Flake v. Neumiller & Beardslee (2017) 9 Cal.App.5th 223.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)
